sections.heading: GitHub Commands
sections.prose: Canonical Git/GitHub command patterns for Contact360 repositories and services.

sections.heading: Initial Push Mapping (Per Codebase)
sections.prose: Use these from each codebase directory.

sections.heading: contact360.io/api -> https://github.com/thekoushikdurgas/appointment360.git (main)
sections.prose:
```bash
git init
git remote add origin https://github.com/thekoushikdurgas/appointment360.git
git branch -M main
git add .
git commit -m "backend deployment v1"
git push -u origin main
```

code_blocks.value:
git init
git remote add origin https://github.com/thekoushikdurgas/appointment360.git
git branch -M main
git add .
git commit -m "backend deployment v1"
git push -u origin main

sections.heading: ~~contact360.io/jobs~~ (archived)
sections.prose:
The standalone Python job scheduler was **removed** from the monorepo. Async jobs are implemented under **`EC2/email.server`**, **`EC2/sync.server`**, and the gateway **`jobs`** module. The historical remote below is kept only for reference if you still maintain a legacy fork:

`https://github.com/thekoushikdurgas/tkdjob.git`

sections.heading: Small Task Breakdown (Execution Plan)
sections.prose:
1. Verify target folder and intended remote URL for one codebase.
2. Run `git init` and set `origin`.
3. Rename/create target branch with `git branch -M <branch>`.
4. Stage and commit using the mapped message for that codebase.
5. Push using `git push -u origin <branch>`.
6. Repeat for the next codebase.

items.text: Verify target folder and intended remote URL for one codebase.
Stage and commit using the mapped message for that codebase.

sections.heading: Safety Checks (Before Running)
sections.prose:
- If a repo already has a remote, use `git remote set-url origin <url>` instead of `git remote add origin ...`.
- If commit fails due to missing identity, set local identity in that repo:
  - `git config user.name "<name>"`
  - `git config user.email "<email>"`
- If branch already exists on remote, push after checking divergence:
  - `git fetch origin`
  - `git status`

items.text: If a repo already has a remote, use `git remote set-url origin <url>` instead of `git remote add origin ...`.
If commit fails due to missing identity, set local identity in that repo:

sections.heading: Branch Workflow
sections.prose:
```bash
git checkout -b feature/<short-name>
git status
git add .
git commit -m "feat: <summary>"
git push -u origin feature/<short-name>
```

code_blocks.value:
git checkout -b feature/<short-name>
git status
git add .
git commit -m "feat: <summary>"
git push -u origin feature/<short-name>

sections.heading: Keep Branch Updated
sections.prose:
```bash
git fetch origin
git checkout main
git pull origin main
git checkout feature/<short-name>
git rebase main
```

code_blocks.value:
git fetch origin
git checkout main
git pull origin main
git checkout feature/<short-name>
git rebase main

sections.heading: Pull Request Commands (GitHub CLI)
sections.prose:
```bash
gh pr create --title "<title>" --body "<summary>"
gh pr status
gh pr view --web
```

code_blocks.value:
gh pr create --title "<title>" --body "<summary>"
gh pr status
gh pr view --web

sections.heading: Review and Merge
sections.prose:
```bash
gh pr checks
gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch
```

code_blocks.value:
gh pr checks
gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch

sections.heading: Tag and Release
sections.prose:
```bash
git checkout main
git pull origin main
git tag vX.Y.Z
git push origin vX.Y.Z
gh release create vX.Y.Z --title "vX.Y.Z" --notes "<release notes>"
```

code_blocks.value:
git checkout main
git pull origin main
git tag vX.Y.Z
git push origin vX.Y.Z
gh release create vX.Y.Z --title "vX.Y.Z" --notes "<release notes>"

sections.heading: Service Repository Mapping (Typical)
sections.prose:
- `contact360.io/app`, `contact360.io/root`, `contact360.io/admin`, `contact360.io/api`, `contact360.io/sync`, `contact360.io/jobs`
- `lambda/emailapis`, `lambda/emailapigo`, `lambda/logs.api`, `lambda/s3storage`
- `backend(dev)/contact.ai`, `backend(dev)/salesnavigator`, `backend(dev)/mailvetter`, `backend(dev)/email campaign`
- `extension/contact360`

sections.heading: Standard PR Flow (All Codebases)
sections.prose:
```bash
git checkout -b feature/<scope>-<short-name>
git add .
git commit -m "feat(<scope>): <summary>"
git push -u origin feature/<scope>-<short-name>
gh pr create --title "feat(<scope>): <summary>" --body "<details>"
gh pr checks
```

code_blocks.value:
git checkout -b feature/<scope>-<short-name>
git add .
git commit -m "feat(<scope>): <summary>"
git push -u origin feature/<scope>-<short-name>
gh pr create --title "feat(<scope>): <summary>" --body "<details>"
gh pr checks

sections.heading: Hotfix Flow
sections.prose:
```bash
git checkout main
git pull origin main
git checkout -b hotfix/<issue-id>
git add .
git commit -m "fix: <hotfix summary>"
git push -u origin hotfix/<issue-id>
gh pr create --title "fix: <hotfix summary>" --body "<impact and rollback>"
```

code_blocks.value:
git checkout main
git pull origin main
git checkout -b hotfix/<issue-id>
git add .
git commit -m "fix: <hotfix summary>"
git push -u origin hotfix/<issue-id>
gh pr create --title "fix: <hotfix summary>" --body "<impact and rollback>"

sections.heading: Release Branch Flow (Optional)
sections.prose:
```bash
git checkout main
git pull origin main
git checkout -b release/vX.Y.Z
git push -u origin release/vX.Y.Z
gh pr create --title "release: vX.Y.Z" --body "<release scope>"
```

code_blocks.value:
git checkout main
git pull origin main
git checkout -b release/vX.Y.Z
git push -u origin release/vX.Y.Z
gh pr create --title "release: vX.Y.Z" --body "<release scope>"

sections.heading: Small Task Breakdown
sections.prose:
1. Create branch from `main`.
2. Commit by service scope (`api`, `jobs`, `app`, `lambda-*`).
3. Open PR with release/stage linkage.
4. Run checks and resolve failures.
5. Merge with approved strategy (`squash` by default).
6. Tag and publish release notes.

items.text: Merge with approved strategy (`squash` by default).

sections.heading: Guardrails
sections.prose:
- Do not use force push to protected branches.
- Do not commit secrets.
- Keep version tags aligned with `docs/version-policy.md` and `docs/versions.md`.

items.text: Do not use force push to protected branches.
Keep version tags aligned with `docs/version-policy.md` and `docs/versions.md`.

sections.heading: 1) Prepare
sections.prose:
- Branch from updated `main`.
- Scope commits by impacted service area.

items.text: Scope commits by impacted service area.

sections.heading: 2) Validate
sections.prose:
- Run local test/lint commands for changed services.
- Ensure docs updates are included when contracts changed.

items.text: Run local test/lint commands for changed services.
Ensure docs updates are included when contracts changed.

sections.heading: 3) PR and Review
sections.prose:
- Open PR with roadmap/version mapping context.
- Ensure checks pass and review comments are resolved.

items.text: Open PR with roadmap/version mapping context.
Ensure checks pass and review comments are resolved.

sections.heading: 4) Merge and Release
sections.prose:
- Merge via approved strategy.
- Create tag/release where applicable.

sections.heading: 5) Post-Merge
sections.prose:
- Confirm deployment or release pipeline status.
- Capture release evidence in checklist docs.

items.text: Confirm deployment or release pipeline status.
Capture release evidence in checklist docs.
